Paragraph

For any partition \(\cP_{1}: 0=s_{0} < s_{1} < \cdots < s_{k}=1 \) of \([0, 1]\) in the \(x\) variable, \(U(f, \cP_{1})=1\) and \(L(f, \cP_{1})=0\text{.}\) Thus \(\lowint_{[0,1]}\, f(x)\, dx =0\) , \(\upint_{[0, 1]}\, f(x)\, dx =1\text{,}\) and \(f\) is not integrable on \([0, 1]\text{.}\)
in-context
